menu_note:
🏢 pressure · ❤️ conscience · 👥 team · 📈 career
If any meter empties or overflows, it's over.
info_title: Are you experiencing mobbing in real life?
info_body:
Mobbing is systematic psychological harassment at work: isolation, humiliation, impossible workloads, rumors.

What helps:
• Keep a dated journal of every incident (what, who, witnesses).
• Save emails and messages — forward them to a private address.
• Report through official channels in writing.
• Talk to a doctor — health effects are real and documentable.
• Consult a labor lawyer; in many countries mobbing is legally recognized.
• You are not alone, and it is not your fault.

This game is fiction, but the patterns are real. If they feel familiar, take them seriously.
end_b0_b: The board adores you. Do you adore the person in the mirror? You teach the methods now.
end_b100_b: “You didn't fit the culture.” Pack your box. But your head is high — you never became one of them.
end_v0_b: One morning the mirror showed the General Manager. Even your voice sounds like his now. Technically, you are still alive.
end_v100_b: Your conscience won. You resigned. The team applauded as you walked out. Maybe you are the real winner.
end_e0_b: Nobody is left. You sit alone in an empty office. Management billed the disaster to you.
end_e100_b: The team loved you so much that management wrote you up for 'failing to maintain authority'. Being loved can be an offense here.
end_k0_b: Your file now reads 'problem manager'. Word got around the industry. But which word — that was always your choice.
end_k100_b: You're upper management now. Tomorrow a file lands on your desk: 'Apply pressure to the lower floors.' The wheel turns. You are the wheel.
bribe_flavor: The envelope slides under the table. The problem ’resolves’.
notif_bodies: 🔔 Someone is crying in the archive room.
end_h0_b: Your body filed the resignation your mind kept postponing. Panic attacks, sleepless nights, a heart that raced at every email tone. The company had a replacement by Friday. You're still trying to find yourself.
end_lawsuit_b: You kept every email, every roster, every witness. In court the polished excuses fell apart. You won — compensation, and your name cleared. The wheel can be stopped. You stopped it.
lawsuit_offer_t: You've gathered enough evidence — emails, rosters, witnesses. Your lawyer says the case is solid. File the lawsuit, or swallow it and carry on?
legal_intro: The real-world weight of your decisions:
legal_sag: %1$d health & safety violations — administrative fines, possible criminal liability
legal_izo: %1$d acts of systematic isolation — legally recognized mobbing (damages)
legal_itb: %1$d blows to dignity & reputation — grounds for moral damages
legal_is: %1$d abusive work practices — bad-faith conduct, unjust treatment
legal_outro: Mobbing is a real workplace offense. If it's happening to you, keep records — and know your rights.
end_caught_b: IT was watching all along. They found the screenshots, the saved emails, the recordings. Security walked you out within the hour — and every file you'd gathered vanished from the server. No evidence, no case. Just a name on a blacklist.
